mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months signals rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months signals rising supply and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months signals falling demand and deflation expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in low-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate over the past 3 months signals a loosening labor market, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-unemployment periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate and a 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months signal a tightening monetary cycle and rising inflation expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1% increase in 3 months and CPIAUCSL > 2% increase in 6 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
